Prepares model to have sampling accelerated via splitting work units.

SelectModelDevice.name: SelectModelDevice
SelectModelDevice.description:
Place the diffusion model on a specific device (default / cpu / gpu:N).

- "default" restores the device assigned by the loader (even after a
  prior Select Model Device call).
- "cpu" pins both the load and offload device to CPU.
- "gpu:N" pins the load device to the Nth available GPU; the offload
  device is restored to the loader's original choice.

When the requested device differs from the device the input model is
already on, a fresh model is spawned via the loader's reload factory
(cached_patcher_init) so the new patcher owns independent weights on
the new device. Loaders that don't support multigpu (no factory) will
cause the node to pass through unchanged with a warning.

If the workflow already has MultiGPU CFG Split applied and the chosen
GPU collides with one of the existing multigpu clones, that clone is
dropped so two patchers don't end up bound to the same device.

When the selected device does not exist on the current machine
(e.g. a workflow built on a 2-GPU box opened on a 1-GPU box),
the node passes the model through unchanged and logs a message
instead of failing.

NOTE: Placing Select Model Device *after* a node that has already
consumed the same model (e.g. a KSampler that ran on this model on
the original device) is not recommended -- any state the prior
consumer mutated on the original model will be observed when the
selected device matches the original (fast path). Place Select Model
Device before any consumer of the model.

SelectCLIPDevice.name: SelectCLIPDevice
SelectCLIPDevice.description:
Place the CLIP text encoder on a specific device (default / cpu / gpu:N).

- "default" restores the device assigned by the loader.
- "cpu" pins both the load and offload device to CPU.
- "gpu:N" pins the load device to the Nth available GPU.

When the selected device does not exist on the current machine
(e.g. a workflow built on a 2-GPU box opened on a 1-GPU box),
the node passes the CLIP through unchanged and logs a message
instead of failing.

SelectVAEDevice.name: SelectVAEDevice
SelectVAEDevice.description:
Place the VAE on a specific device (default / gpu:N).

- "default" restores the device assigned by the loader.
- "gpu:N" pins the load device to the Nth available GPU; the offload
  device is set to the standard VAE offload device.

CPU is intentionally not exposed in the UI for the VAE; if a workflow
supplies "cpu" anyway (e.g. opened from another machine), the request
is dropped with a log message and the VAE is passed through unchanged.

When the selected device does not exist on the current machine
(e.g. a workflow built on a 2-GPU box opened on a 1-GPU box),
the node passes the VAE through unchanged and logs a message
instead of failing.

BerniniConditioning.name: BerniniConditioning
BerniniConditioning.description: Conditioning node for Bernini in-context video/image conditioning. batch_size.tooltip: The number of latent images in the batch.

VoxelToMesh.description: Converts a voxel grid to a mesh.

eta.tooltip: Controls the scale of the parallel guidance vector. Default CFG behavior at a setting of 1.

momentum.tooltip: Controls a running average of guidance during diffusion, disabled at a setting of 0.

RegexReplace.description: Find and replace text using regex patterns.

ReferenceLatent.name: ReferenceLatent
ReferenceLatent.description: This node sets the guiding latent for an edit model. If the model supports it you can chain multiple to set multiple reference images.

model.tooltip: The model to apply context windows to during sampling.

context_length.tooltip: The length of the context window.

context_overlap.tooltip: The overlap of the context window.

context_schedule.tooltip: Step-dependent scheduling algorithm for context windows.

context_stride.tooltip: The stride of the context window; only applicable to uniform schedules.

closed_loop.tooltip: Whether to close the context window loop; only applicable to looped schedules.

fuse_method.tooltip: The method to use to fuse the context windows.

dim.tooltip: The dimension to apply the context windows to.

freenoise.tooltip: Whether to apply FreeNoise noise shuffling, improves window blending.

cond_retain_index_list.tooltip: List of latent indices to retain in the conditioning tensors for each window. For concat-style I2V models (e.g. Wan I2V, HunyuanVideo I2V, Cosmos I2V, SVD) the encoded start image lives in the c_concat conditioning channels; setting this to '0' will retain that start image content at sub-pos 0 of every window.

split_conds_to_windows.tooltip: Whether to split multiple conditionings (created by ConditionCombine) to each window based on region index.

latent_retain_index_list.tooltip: List of latent indices to retain in the noise latent itself for each window. Use for workflows where reference content (e.g. a start image) lives directly in the noise latent rather than in separate conditioning channels (e.g. inplace-style I2V like LTXV, AnimateDiff). Independent of cond_retain_index_list.

causal_window_fix.tooltip: Whether to add a causal fix frame to non-0-indexed context windows.

output_tooltips: The model with context windows applied during sampling.

nerf_tile_size.tooltip: Allows overriding the default NeRF tile size. -1 means use the default (32). 0 means use non-tiling mode (may require a lot of VRAM).

force_sequential_txt_ids.tooltip: Force usage of sequential text token IDs instead of zeroes. Should be used for checkpoints from 2026-05-22 to 2026-06-01 that are trained in this way but do not contain the __sequential__ key in the state dict.

ChromaRadianceOptions.name: ChromaRadianceOptions
ChromaRadianceOptions.description: Allows setting advanced options for the Chroma Radiance model.

latent_format.tooltip: Flux1 (16-ch) and Flux2 (128-ch) latents are auto-detected from channel dim under 'flux'. For SD3 (16-ch), SDXL (4-ch), or QwenImage (16-ch), select manually.

degrade_sigma.tooltip: 0 = clean latent. Increase to denoise corrupted latent outputs.

strength_start.tooltip: Control strength at the start of sampling (high sigma).

strength_end.tooltip: Control strength at the end of sampling (low sigma). Linearly interpolated from start.

restore_cfg.tooltip: Pulls denoised output toward the input latent. Higher = stronger fidelity to input. 0 to disable.

restore_cfg_s_tmin.tooltip: Sigma threshold below which restore_cfg is disabled.

LazyCache.name: LazyCache
LazyCache.description: A homebrew version of EasyCache - even 'easier' version of EasyCache to implement. Overall works worse than EasyCache, but better in some rare cases AND universal compatibility with everything in ComfyUI.

ScaleROPE.name: ScaleROPE
ScaleROPE.description: Scale and shift the ROPE of the model.

ComfyNotNode.name: ComfyNotNode
ComfyNotNode.description: Logical NOT operation. Returns true if the value is falsy. Uses Python's rules for truthiness.

ComfyAndNode.name: ComfyAndNode
ComfyAndNode.description: Logical AND operation. Returns true if all of the values are truthy. Uses Python's rules for truthiness.

ComfyOrNode.description: Logical OR operation. Returns true if any of the values are truthy. Uses Python's rules for truthiness.

megapixels.tooltip: Target total megapixels. 1.0 MP ≈ 1024x1024 for square.

multiple.tooltip: Nearest multiple of the result to set the selected resolution to.

Calculated height in pixels multiplied by the selected multiple.

wanBlockSwap.name: wanBlockSwap

NormalizeVideoLatentStart.description: Normalizes the initial frames of a video latent to match the mean and standard deviation of subsequent reference frames. Helps reduce differences between the starting frames and the rest of the video.

mid_y.tooltip: Normalized Y control point for Bezier curve. Only used when 'bezier' is enabled.

interpolation.tooltip: Controls the timing/speed of movement along the path.

track_mask.tooltip: Optional mask to indicate visible frames.

num_frame_per_block.tooltip: Frames per autoregressive block. 1 = framewise, 3 = chunkwise. Must match the checkpoint's training mode.

EmptyHiDreamO1LatentImage.description: Empty pixel-space latent for HiDream-O1-Image. The model was trained at ~4 megapixels; lower resolutions go off-distribution and quality regresses noticeably. Trained resolutions: 2048x2048, 2304x1728, 1728x2304, 2560x1440, 1440x2560, 2496x1664, 1664x2496, 3104x1312, 1312x3104, 2304x1792, 1792x2304.

images.tooltip: Reference images. 1 image = instruction edit; 2-10 images = multi reference.

HiDreamO1ReferenceImages.name: HiDreamO1ReferenceImages
HiDreamO1ReferenceImages.description: Attach 1-10 reference images to conditioning, one for edit instructionor multiple for subject-driven personalization.

start_percent.tooltip: Sampling progress (0=start, 1=end) at which the blend turns ON.

end_percent.tooltip: Sampling progress at which the blend turns OFF.

pattern.tooltip: Shift layout. single_shift: one pass at the natural patch grid + others offset. symmetric: all passes off-grid, shifts split around origin.

passes.tooltip: Number of passes per gated step. 2/4 = fixed. ramp_*: pass count increases as sampling approaches end (more smoothing where seams are most visible).

blend.tooltip: average: equal-weight mean. window: Hann-windowed weighting favoring each pass away from its patch boundaries. median: per-pixel median, rejects wraparound-outlier passes.

strength.tooltip: Interpolation between the natural-grid pred (0) and the averaged result (1).

HiDreamO1PatchSeamSmoothing.name: HiDreamO1PatchSeamSmoothing
HiDreamO1PatchSeamSmoothing.description: Average the model output across multiple shifted patch-grid positions during the late portion of sampling. Cancels seams.

fov_x_degrees.tooltip: Horizontal field of view of the source camera. Sets the focal length used to unproject the depth map into 3D. 0 = auto-recover from the predicted points.

batch_size.tooltip: Images per inference call. Lower if you OOM on a long video / image set.

apply_mask.tooltip: Set masked-out (sky / invalid) pixels to inf in points and depth so meshing culls them. Disable to keep the raw predicted geometry everywhere; the mask is still returned separately.

image.tooltip: Equirectangular panorama (any aspect).

merge_resolution.tooltip: Long-side resolution of the merged equirect distance map.

MoGePanoramaInference.description: Run MoGe on an equirectangular panorama by splitting it into 12 perspective views, running inference on each, and merging the results into a single depth map.

output.tooltip: DirectX vs OpenGL controls the normal-map green-channel convention. DirectX: green = -Y down (Unreal). OpenGL: green = +Y up (Blender, Substance, Unity, glTF).

discontinuity_threshold.tooltip: Drop pixels whose 3x3 depth span exceeds this fraction. 0 = off.

texture.tooltip: Carry the source image through as the baseColor texture.

MoGePointMapToMesh.description: Convert a MoGe point map into a 3D mesh.

detector_variant.tooltip: Face detector range. 'short' is tuned for close-up faces (within ~2 m of the camera); 'full' covers farther / smaller faces (up to ~5 m) but is slower. 'both' runs both detectors and keeps whichever found more faces per frame (~2× detection cost).

num_faces.tooltip: Maximum faces to return per frame. 0 = no cap (return all detected).

min_confidence.tooltip: BlazeFace score threshold. Lower to catch small/occluded faces.

missing_frame_fallback.tooltip: Per-frame behaviour when detection fails in a batch. 'empty' leaves the frame faceless. 'previous' copies the most recent successful detection. 'interpolate' lerps landmarks/bbox/blendshapes between bracketing successful frames. Multi-face: pairs faces across frames by greedy bbox-centre NN.

MediaPipeFaceLandmarker.name: MediaPipeFaceLandmarker
MediaPipeFaceLandmarker.description: Detects facial landmarks using MediaPipe model.

connections.tooltip: 'all' = oval+eyes+brows+lips+irises+nose. 'fill' = solid face_oval polygon (silhouette mask). 'custom' = toggle each feature individually (including 'tesselation', the full 2547-edge wireframe).

thickness.tooltip: Edge line thickness in pixels. 0 disables edge drawing.

point_size.tooltip: Landmark dot radius in pixels. 0 disables point drawing.

image.tooltip: If not connected, a black canvas will be used.

regions.tooltip: 'all' = union of face_oval+lips+eyes+irises (which collapses to face_oval since it encloses the rest). 'custom' = toggle each region individually for combos like lips+eyes.

face_oval.tooltip: Include the 'face_oval' region in the mask.

lips.tooltip: Include the 'lips' region in the mask.

left_eye.tooltip: Include the 'left_eye' region in the mask.

right_eye.tooltip: Include the 'right_eye' region in the mask.

irises.tooltip: Include the 'irises' region in the mask.

MediaPipeFaceMask.name: MediaPipeFaceMask
MediaPipeFaceMask.description: Draws a mask from face landmarks.

format.tooltip: ply: standard 3D Gaussian Splat with full spherical harmonics. ksplat: mkkellogg SplatBuffer (level 0, uncompressed), base color only spz: Niantic gzip-compressed (~10x smaller), base color only

SplatToFile3D.name: SplatToFile3D
SplatToFile3D.description: Serialize a gaussian splat to a File3D object for Save / Preview 3D nodes. Supports one item per batch only.

File3DToSplat.name: File3DToSplat
File3DToSplat.description: Parse a splat File3D into a gaussian splat. Inverse of Create 3D File (from Splat). Supported format:  PLY, SPLAT, KSPLAT, SPZ. PLY carries full spherical harmonics, the other formats are base color only. Format is auto-detected from the file contents.

frames.tooltip: -1, 0, 1 = single still image; >1 = turntable, the camera orbits over a full 360 turn (works with any camera_info). Negative value orbits the other way.

splat_scale.tooltip: Multiplier on each splat's projected footprint (lower = crisper points, higher = softer/fuller surface).

sharpen.tooltip: Sharpen overlapping splats: 1.0 = physically-correct blend; higher biases each pixel toward its dominant (nearest) splat for crisper texture, without shrinking splats or opening gaps. Non-physical above 1.

headlight_shading.tooltip: Diffuse shading from a light at the camera (headlight), using the splat surfel normals: darkens surfaces that turn away from view to reveal form/curvature. 0 = flat albedo, 1 = strongest shading.

opacity_threshold.tooltip: Cull gaussians with opacity below this (removes faint floaters).

render_style.tooltip: What the image output shows: color, clay (neutral-albedo shaded), depth (near=bright), normal (OpenGL normal map).

bg_image.tooltip: Optional background plate composited behind the splat (overrides the solid background colour). Resized to the render size; a batch is used per frame, a single image for all. color/clay only.

camera_info.tooltip: Camera to render from - a Load3D / Preview3D camera or a Create Camera Info node. If empty, the splat is auto-framed from a default 3/4 view.

RenderSplat.name: RenderSplat
RenderSplat.description: Render a gaussian splat as an image with an anisotropic EWA rasterizer (oriented elliptical splats, antialiased, depth-sorted front-to-back). The camera comes from a camera_info input (Load / Preview 3D, or a Create Camera Info node); leave it empty to auto-frame the splat. Set frames greater than 1 for a turntable batch of images to feed a Video node.

mode.tooltip: How to define the camera: orbit angles, an explicit position, or a position + quaternion.

position_x.tooltip: Camera position in world space (right-handed, Y-up).
Camera position in world space (right-handed, Y-up).

quat_w.tooltip: Camera world-rotation quaternion (three.js: looks down local -Z). Normalized for you.

target_x.tooltip: Look-at point (orbit pivot / aim). In orbit mode, move it to pan/translate the whole camera. Ignored in quaternion mode. Defaults to the origin.

roll.tooltip: Camera roll about the view axis, degrees.

fov.tooltip: Vertical field of view in degrees.

zoom.tooltip: Digital zoom (focal-length multiplier). >1 zooms in without moving the camera.

camera_type.tooltip: Projection used by Render Splat: perspective (foreshortening) or orthographic (parallel).

CreateCameraInfo.name: CreateCameraInfo
CreateCameraInfo.description: Build a camera_infoMode 'orbit' aims with yaw/pitch/distance around the target; 'look_at' places the camera at world position. Coordinates are the viewer's world space (right-handed,Y-up).

TransformSplat.name: TransformSplat
TransformSplat.description: Translate, rotate, and scale a gaussian splat. Non-uniform scale also reshapes every individual splat, slower process.

GetSplatCount.name: GetSplatCount
GetSplatCount.description: Returns the number of splats summed across the batch.

MergeSplat.name: MergeSplat
MergeSplat.description: Concatenate any number of gaussian splats into one. Unioning several decodes of the same latent at different seeds densifies the surface, this can improve surface quality when meshing.

resolution.tooltip: Density-grid resolution along the longest axis. Higher = finer surface, more VRAM/time (grows with resolution^3).

kernel.tooltip: Max splat half-width in voxels. Each gaussian is rasterized over a window sized to its own 3-sigma, capped here - small surfels stay cheap, large ones aren't truncated. Raise if sparse splats leave gaps.

smooth.tooltip: Taubin mesh-smoothing iterations. Smooths the surface without shrinking it (volume-preserving), unlike blurring the density. 0 = raw surface.

level.tooltip: Iso-surface level. Auto-picked by Otsu; this biases it (1.0 = auto, lower = fatter/more-connected surface, higher = thinner/tighter).

min_component.tooltip: Drop connected components smaller than this many vertices (0 = keep all). Removes detached floater blobs and the inner shell of the double wall.

min_opacity.tooltip: Ignore gaussians fainter than this before meshing.

color_sharpen.tooltip: Crisp up the vertex texture: 1.0 = physically-correct blend; higher biases each voxel's colour toward its dominant gaussian instead of averaging neighbours (de-smears the texture). Colour only - geometry is unchanged.

SplatToMesh.name: SplatToMesh
SplatToMesh.description: Extract a coloured mesh from a gaussian splat.

erode_radius.tooltip: Erode the alpha matte by this pixel radius before cropping (avoids border bleed).

size.tooltip: Square image size. The model is trained at 1024; other sizes run but are off-distribution.

TripoSplatPreprocessImage.name: TripoSplatPreprocessImage
TripoSplatPreprocessImage.description: Crop center each image to a square canvas on a black background and add padding.

TripoSplatConditioning.name: TripoSplatConditioning
TripoSplatConditioning.description: Encode the image with DINOv3 and the Flux2 VAE into TripoSplat positive/negative conditioning, and create the fixed size noise target (latent + camera) for the KSampler

num_gaussians.tooltip: Number of gaussians to produce (rounded to a multiple of 32). 262144 matches the octree's point density; higher oversamples the same points (denser, but no new detail) and costs proportionally more VRAM/time.

seed.tooltip: Seeds the octree point sampler (global RNG) for deterministic decodes.

VAEDecodeTripoSplat.name: VAEDecodeTripoSplat
VAEDecodeTripoSplat.description: Decode the sampled TripoSplat latent into a 3D gaussian splat. Modify the number of gaussians to vary the density.

octree_level.tooltip: Octree depth for the preview decode (lower = cheaper/coarser).

num_gaussians.tooltip: Number of gaussians to produce for the preview (rounded to a multiple of 32).

point_size.tooltip: Maximum splat radius in pixels. Each gaussian is sized from its scale and capped here; lower = finer/pointier, higher = chunkier.

TripoSplatSamplingPreview.name: TripoSplatSamplingPreview
TripoSplatSamplingPreview.description: Patch the TripoSplat model for the standard Ksampler node to show a live decoded gaussian splat preview at each step.

resolution.tooltip:
Resolution the model runs at (longest side, multiple of 14).
Lower = faster / less VRAM.
Higher = more detail.
Output is upsampled back to the original size.

resize_method.tooltip:
upper_bound_resize: scale so the longest side = resolution (caps memory, default).
lower_bound_resize: scale so the shortest side = resolution (preserves more detail on tall/wide images, uses more memory).

mode.tooltip:
mono: single view image (works with any model variant).
multiview: all images processed together for geometric consistency + camera pose (for Small/Base models only).

ref_view_strategy.tooltip:
Which view acts as the geometric anchor.
- saddle_balanced: the view most 'average' across all others (best general choice).
- saddle_sim_range: the view most visually distinct from the others.
- first / middle: fixed positional picks.

pose_method.tooltip:
How the camera field-of-view is estimated (for Small/Base models only).
- cam_dec: learned from image features.
- ray_pose: derived geometrically from the model's 3D ray output.
Affects perspective correctness of the 3D output. Try both if results look distorted.

output_tooltips:
Dictionary of non-normalized tensors.
Always has the keys: depth, image, mode.
Optional keys: sky (for Mono/Metric), confidence (for Small/Base), extrinsics + intrinsics (for multi-view).
DA3Inference.name: DA3Inference
DA3Inference.description: Run Depth Anything 3 on an image. In multi-view mode each image is treated as a separate view of the same scene.

output.tooltip:
- depth: normalised greyscale depth image.
- depth_colored: depth mapped through the Turbo colormap.
- sky_mask: sky probability in [0, 1] (for Mono/Metric models only).
- confidence: normalised depth confidence (for Small/Base models only).

normalization.tooltip:
- v2_style: mean/std normalisation for perceptually balanced results (default).
- min_max: stretches the full depth range to [0, 1] for maximum contrast.
- raw: no scaling,preserves metric units for Metric model.

apply_sky_clip.tooltip: Clip sky-region depth to the 99th percentile of foreground depth before normalisation. Requires a sky key in the da3_geometry input (for Mono/Metric models only).

normalization.tooltip:
- v2_style: mean/std normalisation for perceptually balanced results (default).
- min_max: stretches the full depth range to [0, 1] for maximum contrast.
- raw: no scaling,preserves metric units for Metric model.

apply_sky_clip.tooltip: Clip sky-region depth to the 99th percentile of foreground depth before normalisation. Requires a sky key in the da3_geometry input (for Mono/Metric models only).

colored.tooltip: Apply the Turbo colormap to the sky mask.
Apply the Turbo colormap to the confidence map.

DA3Render.name: DA3Render
DA3Render.description: Render a depth map, confidence map, or sky mask from Depth Anything 3 geometry data.

batch_index.tooltip: Which image of a batch to convert. Per-image vertex counts differ so batches cannot be stacked.

decimation.tooltip: Vertex stride. 1 = full resolution, 2 = half, etc.

discontinuity_threshold.tooltip: Drop triangles whose 3x3 depth span exceeds this fraction. 0 = off.

confidence_threshold.tooltip: Exclude pixels whose per-image normalised confidence is below this value (0 = keep all, 1 = keep only the single most confident pixel). Used when the geometry has a confidence map (Small/Base models).

use_sky_mask.tooltip: Exclude sky-probability pixels (sky >= 0.5) from the mesh. Used when the geometry has a sky map (Mono/Metric models).

texture.tooltip: Use the source image as a base color texture.

DA3GeometryToMesh.name: DA3GeometryToMesh
DA3GeometryToMesh.description: Convert a depth map into a triangulated 3D mesh.

thinking_level.tooltip: How hard the model reasons internally before answering. HIGH improves quality on difficult tasks but costs more (thinking) tokens and is slower.

temperature.tooltip: Controls randomness. Lower is more focused/deterministic, higher is more creative.

top_p.tooltip: Nucleus sampling: sample from the smallest token set whose cumulative probability reaches top_p.

max_output_tokens.tooltip: Maximum tokens to generate, including the model's internal thinking. With thinking_level HIGH, a low value can leave no room for the answer; raise this if responses come back empty or truncated. The model stops early when finished, so a higher cap costs nothing extra for short replies.

seed.tooltip: When seed is fixed to a specific value, the model makes a best effort to provide the same response for repeated requests. Deterministic output isn't guaranteed. Also, changing the model or parameter settings, such as the temperature, can cause variations in the response even when you use the same seed value. By default, a random seed value is used.

system_prompt.tooltip: Foundational instructions that dictate an AI's behavior.
system_prompt.default:
You are an expert image-generation engine. You must ALWAYS produce an image.
Interpret all user input—regardless of format, intent, or abstraction—as literal visual directives for image composition.
If a prompt is conversational or lacks specific visual details, you must creatively invent a concrete visual scenario that depicts the concept.
Prioritize generating the visual representation above any text, formatting, or conversational requests.

RunwayAleph2VideoToVideoNode.name: RunwayAleph2VideoToVideoNode
RunwayAleph2VideoToVideoNode.description: Edit a video with a text prompt using Runway's Aleph2 model. Aleph2 transforms your footage (restyle, relight, add or remove elements, change the viewpoint) while keeping the original motion and timing; the output resolution matches the input video, which must be 2-30 seconds at 30 fps or lower. Optionally steer the edit with either keyframes (anchored to the input video) or prompt images (anchored to the output video) - use one or the other, not both.
